This is the unique identifier used to link all 4 files together (RX030 in the Personal Register, PX030 in the Personal Data file, DB030 in the Household Register and HB030 Household Data file).  Every household will receive a household number. This number is the basis on which the Household ID and the Personal ID are constructed.  It is sequential, unique for all years of the survey, and it will be in accordance with confidentiality rules.  Household ID = Household number + split number (2 digits) The split number for the first wave is '00'.  If the household remains intact, it keeps the same household ID from one wave to the next.  If it "splits-off", the initial household will keep the original household ID, and the other household (i.e. the split-off household) will keep the same household number, but will be assigned the next available unique split number in sequence.
processing_anonymisation: Basic variable from original data.

description: Used 2008 onwards.  For each working age person (aged 18 to 64) in the household who is not classified as a dependent, the following are calculated: 1. the number of months in the previous year in which the person has given informaiton about their activity status (the 'workable' months); 2. the number of months in the previous year in which the person has been classified as 'at work'. Work intensity is the value derived from point 2 divided by that from point 1, and the results are defined as follows:  0 - 0.2 Very low work intensity 0.2 - 0.45 Low work intensity 0.45 - 0.55 Medium work intensity 0.55 - 0.85 High work intensity 0.85 - 1 Very high work intensity There is an additional category of 0.2 - 1 which represents work intensity other than very low
Used 2008 onwards.  0 not low work intensity 1 low work intensity 2 not applicable
Used 2008 onwards.  0 not severely deprived 1 severely deprived

variable_title: Number of rooms available to the household
area: Dwelling type, tenure status and housing conditions
description: A room is defined as a space of at least 4m2, with a height of over 2 metres, which is accessible from inside the dwelling.  Bathroom, toilet, corridor, utility room, lobby and veranda are excluded.  If the dwelling is shared by more than one household, and some rooms are shared with other households (within the same dwelling), the number of shared rooms is divided by the number of households and the equal share added to each household.  This variable can therefore be coded with one decimal place.  Discrete values to one decimal place between 1 and 10, where 10 represents 10 or more rooms.
